title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Mastery Check
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an explainable constrained optimization model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: Calculate the determinant of [[6, 1], [2, 9]]. Use the result to decide Optimization Ethics for an explainable constrained optimization model.
steps.why: The Optimization Ethics result for an explainable constrained optimization model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (6 × 9) − (1 × 2). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Optimization Ethics, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for an explainable constrained optimization model.
